turns.user_message: I've finally figured out that I am just completely hollowed out lately. It only started a couple days ago, but everything feels strangely muted now. I tried listening to my usual playlists while walking around, but the melodies just sound like background noise. Even my favorite snacks taste like cardboard and I keep wondering what changed so fast. I guess I am just stuck in this weird gray fog for now.

I keep telling myself that I am just completely hollowed out lately, but it is not fading away like I hoped. Every morning I wake up expecting the spark to return, yet the same dullness just sits heavy in my chest. I try to force myself into old hobbies, but my hands just move without any real interest behind them. The colors around me seem washed out and I am starting to accept that this flatness is my new normal. I just keep waiting for a shift that never actually arrives.

I have to admit that I am completely hollowed out lately, and this heavy emptiness is swallowing everything I used to care about. Nothing pulls me out of it anymore, no matter how much I try to distract myself with bright screens or loud music. I just sit there staring at the walls while my mind feels totally cut off from the world outside. It is exhausting to pretend I am fine when I am just a shell going through the motions. I really do not know how to fill this void when nothing matters anymore.

generation_metadata.diversity_anchor: The speaker opens with a conclusion they've reached about themselves. This just started recently.
